title: Customize calendar widget colors
excerpt: My team is in need of the ability to manually select the color that a board displays as in the Dashboard. To minimize visual distraction, we combined all of our work efforts into one master board, but the color that is a…

posts.author: cdf (Carrie d.)
posts.cookedHtml:
My team is in need of the ability to manually select the color that a board displays as in the Dashboard. To minimize visual distraction, we combined all of our work efforts into one master board, but the color that is auto-assigned (even when I duplicate my board and delete the other one in an effort to change the colors, per the direction of support when I emailed) is almost always a very bright pink/red/purple and is difficult to see/read text on across our users screens, when screensharing via Zoom/Skype, or when projecting in large meetings.

It’d be very helpful to be able to change the settings and select what color each Board that is displaying in the Dashboard (specifically the Calendar View) is, to increase the legibility.

posts.author: mtnmama125 (TIffany Schmid)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes, same need here! Please allow users to set the color of each board to enable much more visually efficient timeline and calendar widget. The randomly assigned colors are mostly dark and not easily discernible from project to project (shades of purple/blue and olive green/gray seem to dominate).
Thanks!

posts.author: HristinaPetkova (Hristina)
posts.cookedHtml: My team is also not pleased with the colour selection - has the option been provided now as I’m still struggling to find it

posts.author: gusz (Gustavo Rosa Silva)
posts.cookedHtml: same here…I have to compare charts in a dashboard but it’s difficult since they all have the same color (column chart)

posts.author: ana-i (Ana Iervolino)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi!
I have chosen to colour code the calendar in the dashboard according to people, and am surprised to see that the same person receive different colours for items that are in different boards. Can we just one colour for each person (regardless of which board the data is coming from)?

posts.author: JordanKilgour (Jordan Kilgour)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, this feature definitely would help us. I don’t know why and how it decides on what colors to select for each person. This is frustrating that we have no control over changing them. Thanks.

posts.author: sabimorr (Sabia Morrison)
posts.cookedHtml: We would love this as well.

posts.author: cbarto (Claire)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes please fix this. Currently our board is a combination of grey, brown with black text (illegible) for our most important calendar, and two very similar pink colors for two different calendars. Not ideal haha.

posts.author: JenSikes (Jen Sikes)
posts.cookedHtml: Having the ability to select colors would help us as well.

posts.author: antonGM (Anton)
posts.cookedHtml:
The fact that this hasn’t been done in 2 years since Jan 2020 is insane to me. Calendar that pulls data from different boards is basically unusable bc the colors that are assigned randomly make not sense to anyone at all.

Please add the manual color selection for items in the calendar board feature ASAP!

posts.author: Aidan (Aidan Leather)
posts.cookedHtml:
Dear Monday gods, pleaaaaaaaaaase add manual colour selection!

I promise, it’ll be the best quick win of 2022 for y’all.

Thanks,
Aidan

posts.author: chbroussard (Chris Broussard)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m a brand new user, just figuring out that this capability doesn’t exist. Definitely a disappointment / frustration. This sort of obvious, minor thing should be a given.

posts.author: kate.bartley (Kate Bartley)
posts.cookedHtml: Another request for this please! Would be very helpful to have

posts.author: BDavis (Brenna Davis)
posts.cookedHtml: @monday-team Can we make customizable color-coding on calendar views happen? 42 votes and 2+ years of people requesting. Thanks!

posts.author: ElliBA (Elinor Bohlin Andersson)
posts.cookedHtml: @monday-team any update on this? There’s been a lot of radio silence from you on this topic.

posts.author: jcrab (Jillian Crab)
posts.cookedHtml:
Given the customization available for sections/groups and drop-downs within boards, it’s VERY hard to believe that this has not been addressed yet. My calendar is auto selecting orange and red for my two main projects which makes me thing they are all on fire/running late. Another project is a dark brown with black text – I saw another user mention they have that as well – and it is really difficult to read.

Please make manual color selection available for calendar dashboards!! Second option would be manually selecting colors for boards that can are reflected across the whole monday interface/widgets.

posts.author: LEnglish (Lee English)
posts.cookedHtml: As a brand new customer, it’s extremely disappointing that such a seemingly simple feature has not only not been addressed, but has gone unanswered for two and a half years.

posts.author: Donna (Donna Bruce)
posts.cookedHtml: @monday-team is there any update on this?

posts.author: krystel (Krystel)
posts.cookedHtml: @monday-team any update?

posts.author: amillssalvation (Amy Mills)
posts.cookedHtml: Monday.com, so disappointing to see that you are not addressing this. I need to be able to change a person’s color on the calendar view. Ugh.

posts.author: twilliams (Taylor Williams)
posts.cookedHtml: Color coding is crucial to our efficiency, and the options that are auto-assigned are certainly not ADA compliant. PLEASE FIX

posts.author: DamcoSolutions ( Neelam Bhatt)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @cdf !

**First of all, I would like to welcome you to the monday community!

To manually select & change the colors of ‘Dashboard Calendar View’, you need to click the HEX Color Code or the RGB Color Code option from your design tab in monday dot com. This will open a pop-up window where you can choose colors of your interest. By clicking apply option, you can change the color codes of your calendar dashboards. With color coding, you don’t have to create separate calendar boards for each projects and can easily discern activities based on colors. Experts with rich knowledge of Calendly monday integration can apply color codes to the calendar dashboards and deliver much more visually effective calendar widget for your team.

Regards
Neelam B
Damco Solutions
Phone: +1 609 632 0350 | Email : info@damcogroup.com

posts.author: LanceV (Lance Veloso)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you for this solution! 1 question though, where can we find the Design tab to choose and apply the color for the boards in calendar widget. Is it from an app or template? Thanks in advance!

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml: Would love to have the possibility to use different color setting in timeline view and the timeline widget. Currently the colors are based on the group color and I want them to use a status column (like green is done, red is overdue and so on). Both timeline view and timeline widget has a Group by … setting and the request is to add a Color by … setting which allows selecting a Status column.

posts.author: claybustin (Clay)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes. I’m new to monday.com but this is one of the first limitations I have come across!

posts.author: Emmanilsson (Emma Nilsson)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, this would be SO great! Our team find it hard to use the monday timeline without this feature…

posts.author: cfernandez (Carlos Fernández Carvajal)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all

This is a feature several customers are asking for. I don’t know why is no voted because is quite requested.

Do someone have some workaround but duplicate groups into the same board or duplicate boards and use dashboards?

Regards!

posts.author: Dupre (Guillaume Dupre)
posts.cookedHtml: It would be tremendously helpful. The timeline is almost useless if you can’t see directly from it the status of each task.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone,

We sincerely apologise for the delay in responding to this thread, and recognise it has been a significant amount of time since we’ve updated this request! We are working on improving our responsiveness to the community, particularly our feature request process so appreciate your patience in advance!

On that note, as we pull together all the votes associated with this topic, we would love if you can redirect the votes on this thread, to the primary thread (most voted) here: Manually choose color-coding for boards displayed in Dashboard calendar view - #17 by jcrab - as we plan to close this smaller thread in an attempt to consolidate all votes, and better prioritise this feature request based upon the total amount of votes

Thank you!

posts.author: JTC (Josh)
posts.cookedHtml:
Our team uses Monday.com to plan and manage upcoming events. Each event is a separate pulse in our event planning board. We use the calendar view a lot, both on a weekly and monthly to quickly see what events are upcoming and we like that we can drill down into the event from that view.

In the board view calendar, the color of the pulse is defined by which group it is in. In the calendar dashboard widget, the color of the pulse is defined by which board the pulse is in (color is assigned at random). In both cases we would like the ability to choose a status column that defines the color of the pulse in the calendar.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @JTC, Josh

This is similar to Color coding timeline (and widget) with status color. There are more of these request, please thumb up the other ones

posts.author: aerolito (hugo)
posts.cookedHtml: We manage a lot of data in Dashboard and this feature will be very awesome to avoid time consuming

posts.author: JTC (Josh)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Bas de Bruin,
Yes, very similar to your request. Would be great to see both happen

posts.author: MelissaC (Melissa Cheney)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello all- Melissa from monday here!

I think this is a great idea, and we are looking at the possibility of doing this internally. What you can do now within the dashboard is ‘color by’ and color by a specific column of your choosing. I understand this doesn’t get exactly what you are looking for, but hopefully this will help!

Screen Shot 2021-05-19 at 8.47.27 AM2844×1144 340 KB

posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all,

We are really sorry for the delay in responding to this thread, and recognise it has been a significant amount of time since we’ve updated this thread. We are working on improving our responsiveness to the community, particularly our feature request process so appreciate your patience in advance!

On that note, as we pull together all the votes associated with this topic, we would love if you can redirect the votes on this thread, to the primary thread (most voted) here: Manually choose color-coding for boards displayed in Dashboard calendar view - #17 by jcrab - as we plan to close this smaller thread in an attempt to consolidate all votes, and better prioritise this feature request based upon the total amount of votes

Thank you!

posts.author: mholbert (Madison Holbert)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, for sure need this!! Just finished live chatting with a representative on Monday looking for the answer to this issue… could not believe that there wasn’t already a solution out there! Thought I was just having a hard time locating it. Monday, please fix!

posts.author: CMoss (Chris Mariot)
posts.cookedHtml: Please address this. Not being able to choose a color for each board represented in calendar view is a huge miss IMO. My board colors are pink, red and yellow and it’s driving me crazy.

posts.author: PaulS (Paul Suurmeijer)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would also really like to manually choose the colors, but if we can’t, at least for the automatic colors based on “Color by Board” to be better… I have selected five boards that I’d like to display, and two of them are almost the exact shade of yellow (out of five colors!).

Screenshot 2023-04-14 at 10.40.521672×96 17.1 KB

posts.author: kmcdaniel (Katie McDaniel)
posts.cookedHtml:
I just don’t understand how monday thinks this color

is an effective calendar viewing option. Completely inaccessible.

posts.author: kaylak (Kayla K)
posts.cookedHtml:
My team has a need for this as well. We use the dashboards to combine some of our calendars to help with planning.

When I combine the boards, I color the calendar by board. The colors it choses are random, it does not correlate with a group or status. The colors that default are often hard to read (dark background with black text), too similar to each other (two similar shades of yellow) or not color blind friendly. Being able to chose the colors would help eliminate these issues for our team.

posts.author: katie_e (Katie Edwards)
posts.cookedHtml: This is a wall I have come up against recently. I have a calendar widget that is designed to show my team dates from multiple columns (e.g. start date, end date, action date, etc.). Ideally I want to be able to colour code these based on which column the dates are from but I cannot see this option in the ‘Colour By’ settings - it only offers the options to colour by other status columns when really what I want is for dates from the start date column to be red, dates from the end date column to be green, etc. (for example). It would be fab if this functionality could be added. Thank you!

posts.author: Patrick_B (Patrick)
posts.cookedHtml:
My company is just setting up Monday.com as our CRM and scrolling through this thread and seeing this issue has not been fixed for over 2yrs is worrisome. This is an obvious feature that other calendar providers, such as google, have. Our current calendar in monday differentiates boards using purple, pink, a kinda strawberry red and another lighter purple. To put it plainly…its awful to look at.

Unfortunately, this isnt the first basic feature that ive come across that monday seems to seriously drop the ball on.

posts.author: erioli (Erika)
posts.cookedHtml: It would be nice and helpful if we could assign a different color to each board, so that we can have a colored preview of the projects!

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you for your feedback! After discussing with the relevant team and reading through your feedback, we agree that being able to customize calendar widget colors is an important feature. While we are planning on developing this feature in the future, a the moment we are focusing on other areas of the platform and do not have a definite date for when this feature will be released. We will make sure to keep you update on the development of this feature.

Learn more about how our feature request process works: All you need to know about our new Feature Request process

posts.author: coreywalden (Corey Walden)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m onboarding my team to Monday and it’s exciting, my manager is looking forward to presenting our new dashboard to our VP tomorrow. She just asked if we can change the color of the calendar items to something more appealing. I responded “of course, Monday is super customizable, there has to be a way”. Disappointed and somewhat alarmed to find that this wildly popular and much-needed feature request has not been prioritized in almost 4 years.

posts.author: coreywalden (Corey Walden)
posts.cookedHtml: I don’t understand this. I have the same color on the calendar that I just created, and we’re showing it to our VP tomorrow. This brown is going to look great on her big screen.

posts.author: kmcdaniel (Katie McDaniel)
posts.cookedHtml: It’s an accessibility issue. It should be a priority. Brand Guidelines | Accessibility | Color & Type

posts.author: hkleavitt (Hannah White)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there an update here? It doesn’t look like I can change the automatically assigned colors on a dashboard calendar yet. We got three very similar shade of blue, it’s very confusing. This should definitely be a feature by now.

posts.author: jcavalier (Jaclyn Cavalier)
posts.cookedHtml: I have been trying to find a solution to this same problem! The brown is a massive issue and makes it completely unusable. I just spent an hour on help chat with someone who THOUGHT it could be fixed, but I had to show him this thread that I found showing it’s not possible. I love Monday.com and it’s been amazing to use, but this is the MOST frustrating feature I have come across. Monday.com… PLEASE make this update!

posts.author: jcavalier (Jaclyn Cavalier)
posts.cookedHtml: @Julietteb Can you please give an update on this? This brown color is a huge problem!

posts.author: hkleavitt (Hannah White)
posts.cookedHtml: We’ve had issues with the brown as well! It’s not legible at all against the black text, especially when we project the screen onto a tv/larger screen for the team to discuss on. This should be an easy fix, we should be able to choose what color represents each board. We love Monday but this is so frustrating.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Community, The dashboard team has read through your comments and is planning to develop the ability to customize calendar widget colors and are starting to look into the requirements. At the moment, there is no specific timeline or date for when this feature will be ready, but we will know more soon! You can make sure to be the first to know about new feature by turning on your notifications for this thread. Here is how: How to turn on notifications for a post.

posts.author: mcat (Melissa Catelli)
posts.cookedHtml: We really need to be able to have each board come across as a different color in our dashboard calendars. We have 2/9 boards coming through with the same color in one dashboard and no consistency across dashboards for any of the board colors (the same board will come up as a different color in a different dashboard calendar). Please prioritize this fix!

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Community, I checked in again with the Dashboards team and they explain that while we are planning to develop the ability to customize calendar widget colors in the future, it is not currently in development due to other needs within the dashboards team.

When thinking about the upcoming roadmap, our team needs to make difficult decisions about how we prioritize new features, improve old ones, and ensure overall platform quality.
Although there is no specific timeline or date for when this feature will start development, it is something that the team has in mind for future roadmaps.

As soon as the development teams add this to the official roadmap and starts working on its development, I will update!

Thanks for your dedication and support.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
I spoke with the dashboard team has read through your comments, and while we are planning to develop the ability to customize calendar widget colors in the future, it is not currently in development.

When thinking about the upcoming roadmap, our team needs to make difficult decisions about how we prioritize new features, improve old ones, and ensure overall platform quality.
Although there is no specific timeline or date for when this feature will start development, it is something that the team has in mind for future roadmaps.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Everyone, due to other priorities and new features in development, the ability to customize calendar widget colors is not currently being worked on.

While the feature is not currently planned, our roadmap is subject to change due to evolving capabilities and capacity, so we may be able to add this feature into our future roadmap.

In the meantime, there are some apps or workarounds suggested in the thread that may help solve your needs.

Thanks for all the feedback and I will update you with any changes to this status.

posts.author: smiranda (Santiago Miranda)
posts.cookedHtml: Years have gone by, and we still can’t choose colors in the calendar widget. @monday-team

posts.author: stephglass (Stephanie)
posts.cookedHtml: This is clearly NOT a solution. It’s ridiculous that this extremely basic functionality isn’t possible after years and years!

posts.author: huysgt (HarryNg)
posts.cookedHtml:
Just wanted to share a quick workaround we’ve been using internally for our team to deal with the calendar color issue. It’s not perfect, but it helps make things a bit more readable in the Dashboard view.

Happy to share more if anyone’s interested—curious if it’d be helpful for others too?

via GIPHY

posts.author: hollyvera (Holly Duque)
posts.cookedHtml:
THIS IS A NEED. I have a dashboard that connects multiple boards together (we use the Calendar view) and I just need the calendar to be color coded by board BUT it randomly assigns colors that don’t go great with our brand.

I also think there is a flaw because when you select “One By One” “Color By” - it doesn’t actually allow me to color one by one (for example if for 1 board I want to color by Status but for another board I want to color just by the board…it won’t let me actually do that and it changes it for all). BIG FLAW!!

posts.author: reinier (Reinier ― 📅 Calendar Plus 🍅 Automato)
posts.cookedHtml:
We got this exact question a while back from our customers and also from Holly☝️ in another discussion. You can set a fixed colour per board by setting up a dedicated board colour status column in your board:

automagical.work

Customize colors when coloring by board – automagical.work

Is your monday.com workspace working for you? Complete your workspace with Calendar Plus.

Doable with monday’s own calendar. If you want to pick exact colours (eg. brand colours) you can perform the same trick with the colour picker column type, but you’ll need Calendar Plus to colour by the colour picker column.

posts.author: dan_stargateapps (Dan T.)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello everyone, after reading this thread, I decided to build something that would solve (at least partially) this issue. It’s a small, free app that enables you to change the color of the background and text of the calendar items, as well as their size.

It’s now live on the monday.com marketplace (https://monday.com/marketplace/listing/10000974/custom-calendar-colors), and you can adjust colors directly in your calendar view without having to duplicate boards or rely on the auto-assigned ones.

Enjoy! And please let me know if you have any feedback

posts.author: kookookachoo (Katie K)
posts.cookedHtml:
Chiming in as well - I’m currently suffering with two boards in one calendar view where one is a bright maroon and the other a dark red. I’ve seen some comments of others getting barely legible browns and yellows.

I understand there are persistency and access issues if the color settings are global vs. personal, but a setting for at least personal view color changing is a need. We use this calendar daily. There could even be a “theme”, ex. Maybe I can’t customize board by board but I can pick “primary colors” or a “blue set” for my personal view. The colors that are automatically assigned are not working.

posts.author: Kate.wyeth (Kate)
posts.cookedHtml: I’ve just used this workaround of adding a status column and called it ‘board colour’ then named the status the name of the board and selected my colour. Also set up an automation so that every time an item is created it automatically sets the status. Now by connected board calendar is readable and no more brown!!
